player_news.name: Raya
player_news.notes: Arsenal rotated their entire XI after winning the title, with a Champions League final pending. Raya was NOT in the GW38 matchday squad — confirmed zero minutes. Arteta fielded only 2 survivors from GW37. Starting GK position void; no clean sheet upside from this slot.

player_news.name: Gabriel
player_news.notes: Double jeopardy: squad.json marks Gabriel DOUBTFUL, and confirmed GW38 reports show him on the bench as Arsenal rotated post-title. Saka, Gabriel, Gyokeres and Eze all on a strong-looking bench. Did not start. Treat as a non-contributor.

player_news.name: Gvardiol
player_news.notes: Man City made sweeping changes for a dead rubber. Gvardiol confirmed among substitutes in GW38. Haaland, Donnarumma, Khusanov, Guehi, O'Reilly and Rodri also rested or absent. Guardiola gave fringe players minutes. Gvardiol did not start.

player_news.name: Aina
player_news.notes: Aina began working on the pitch Thursday but was ruled out of Forest's season finale vs Bournemouth. Vitor Pereira explicitly said the side can relax and confirmed no risks with half-fit players. Aina did not make the squad. Confirmed absent.

player_news.name: M.Salah
player_news.notes: Confirmed starter for his Liverpool farewell match — emotional send-off with full motivation. Multiple sources confirm Salah in XI alongside Robertson (also in farewell). Vice-captain designation validated. High-confidence nailed start.

player_news.name: Mbeumo
player_news.notes: Brentford chasing European qualification vs Liverpool on final day — maximum motivation. Mbeumo started and played 74 minutes in a confirmed 3-0 Brentford win, scoring 1 goal. Full-strength XI deployed. Nailed starter with returns confirmed.

player_news.name: Rogers
player_news.notes: Aston Villa won the Europa League final approximately 20 May, days before GW38. Emery confirmed it is difficult to refocus and hinted at squad rotation. Villa play away at Man City with no meaningful league position at stake. Rogers flagged HIGH rotation risk — Emery may manage his minutes.

player_news.name: Anderson
player_news.notes: Confirmed in Forest's predicted GW38 XI vs Bournemouth (4-4-1-1: Sels; Williams, Milenkovic, Morato, Netz; Hutchinson, Anderson, Dominguez, Gibbs-White; Jesus; Wood). No injury concern. Nailed central midfield starter for the season finale.

player_news.name: Bowen
player_news.notes: West Ham in a relegation decider vs Leeds on the final day — maximum motivation. Bowen confirmed in the XI. Squad captain pick is correctly placed here. Two double-figure hauls in his last three home games. Nailed captain-class starter. Over 134,000 FPL managers transferred him in for GW38. Captain call validated.

player_news.name: Mateta
player_news.notes: Confirmed in Crystal Palace's predicted GW38 XI as the number nine. Palace won the Conference League and still have pride to play for. No injury concern flagged. Mateta starts as first-choice striker.

player_news.name: Wood
player_news.notes: Confirmed as Forest's GW38 striker in the predicted XI vs Bournemouth. An earlier-season hip injury scare is resolved. Forest's number one striker for the season finale. Nailed start.

player_news.name: Sels
player_news.notes: Bench GK in the squad. Confirmed starting GK for Nottingham Forest vs Bournemouth in GW38 (in Forest's own predicted XI). CRITICAL auto-sub note: given Raya's confirmed squad omission, Sels is the natural auto-sub GK replacement. If Raya is not in the Arsenal matchday squad, Sels auto-subs into the starting XI. Monitor GK slot carefully.

player_news.name: Haaland
player_news.notes: CRITICAL ALERT: Haaland confirmed NOT in Man City's GW38 matchday squad. Status correctly flagged UNAVAILABLE in squad.json. Bench player — scores zero points. Gemini's captain pick of Haaland is entirely void. Any TC chip activation on Haaland would return zero. The bench penalty applies but no captain multiplier.

player_news.name: Munoz
player_news.notes: Munoz confirmed in Crystal Palace's predicted GW38 XI. Recovered from earlier shoulder injury. Palace have the Conference League trophy and play with pride on final day. Bench DEF option who starts for Palace — useful auto-sub cover if Aina's absence triggers the chain.

player_news.name: Kluivert
player_news.notes: Bournemouth unbeaten run creates motivation but Kluivert came off the bench in mid-week fixtures prior to GW38. One source notes 'it would be a leap for them to start' in the season finale. Bench MID with medium rotation risk — may start from the bench for Bournemouth.

key_alerts: CRITICAL — Haaland UNAVAILABLE (zero points): Confirmed not in Man City's GW38 squad. Gemini's captain pick of Haaland is bust. Squad captain is Bowen (WHU, relegation decider) — this is the correct armband holder. Do NOT activate TC chip.

CRITICAL — Raya NOT IN SQUAD: Arsenal's GW38 rotation was total — Raya omitted from matchday squad entirely. Zero clean sheet coverage from the GK slot. Sels (bench GK) is the auto-sub trigger; Sels IS in Forest's starting XI so auto-sub activates if Raya is confirmed out.

CRITICAL — Aina RULED OUT: Vitor Pereira confirmed no risks with half-fit players for the season finale. Aina did not make the Forest squad. Auto-sub chain from bench: Munoz (CRY DEF, confirmed starting) is the likely replacement DEF.
HIGH — Gabriel DOUBTFUL + BENCHED: Double risk confirmed. Arsenal rotated their entire XI post-title. Gabriel on bench. Did not start. Significant defensive weakness in the live XI.
HIGH — Gvardiol BENCHED: Man City dead rubber rotation confirmed. Gvardiol named among substitutes. No clean sheet upside, limited attacking returns expected.
HIGH — Rogers ROTATION RISK: Aston Villa won the Europa League final days before GW38. Emery hinted at rotation for the trip to Man City. Rogers is not guaranteed to start.
NAILED — Bowen (captain, WHU): West Ham relegation decider vs Leeds. Full-strength guaranteed. Captain pick validated by multiple sources.
NAILED — Salah (vice-captain, LIV): Confirmed starter for farewell match. Sentimental and competitive motivation.
NAILED — Mbeumo (BRE): Started and scored in confirmed 3-0 Brentford win. Brentford chasing Europe.
NAILED — Anderson, Wood, Sels (NFO): All three confirmed in Forest's predicted XI vs Bournemouth.
NAILED — Mateta, Munoz (CRY): Both in Crystal Palace's predicted GW38 XI.
last_minute_risks: GK slot: Confirm Raya's absence in official Arsenal team sheet. If Raya is not in the squad, Sels auto-subs in as starting GK — Forest vs Bournemouth is a clean sheet opportunity.
Captain: Bowen (WHU) is correct captain given Haaland's unavailability. Do NOT allow Gemini's Haaland captain pick to stand. Verify armband assignment before GW38 deadline.
Gabriel: If DOUBTFUL downgrades to OUT before deadline, auto-sub chain activates. Munoz (CRY DEF, confirmed starting) is the likely DEF replacement from bench.
Aina: Treat as OUT. Auto-sub Munoz covers the DEF slot — Munoz is confirmed in Palace's XI so this auto-sub works cleanly.
Rogers: If Emery confirms rotation at the pre-match presser, Rogers does not score. No bench MID cover with reliable starts — flag for manual attention.
Gvardiol: If confirmed bench pre-kickoff, zero clean sheet points and minimal attacking upside. No bench DEF of higher quality available after Munoz covers Aina.
Haaland bench: Dead asset this week. Zero points confirmed. Bench penalty is the only cost.
Bowen / Salah double safety: If Bowen somehow misses out (very unlikely given relegation context), Salah as vice-captain takes the armband. Both nailed.
